title: Veteran Reintegration Counseling - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Diverse Populations > Veteran Reintegration Counseling 12 Matching Service Providers EASTERSEALS OREGON EASTERSEALS OREGON BEND WORK SOURCE HOMELESS VETERAN REINTEGRATION PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Assists veterans in reintegrating into the community and finding employment. A structured, individually designed case management plan may include skill sets such as financial literacy, computer training, job search training, and interview guidance. Veterans learn skills to retain employment and become financially independent. 1007 SW Emkay Drive Bend, OR 97702 Eligibility: Must be homeless or at risk of homelessness (lacking a permanent address), or have been homeless within the last 20 days. Must have at least one day of active duty, must have any discharge other than a dishonorable discharge. Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm Email (541) 414-3680 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MILITARY ONESOURCE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides phone and internet support service through the Department of Defense, offering assistance and resources to military personnel and their families on various issues. Services include counseling and information on personal issues, money matters, career issues, parenting, legal issues, elder care, relocation, violence, trauma, and transition to civilian life. Website Eligibility: Individuals who meet one or more of the following criteria: -Active duty service member of the Army, Marine Corps, Navy, Air Force, or Space Force and their immediate family members. This includes spouses, children, and anyone who has legal resposibility for the active duty service member's children. -Service members of the National Guard and reserve and their immediate family members. This includes spouses, children, and anyone who has legal resposibility for the active duty service member's children. -Coast Guard members who are activated as part of the Department of the Navy under Title 10 authority, as well as their immediate family members -Survivors who have not remarried and children of active-duty, National Guard, and reserve service members (regardless of activation status or cause of service member's death) -Medically discharged service members and their immediate family members currently enrolled in their service branch's wounded warrior or seriously ill and injured program -Retired service members and their immediate family members until 365 days past end of tour of service, retirement date or discharge date, including service members on the Temporary Disability Retirement List. Coast Guard veterans and their immediate family are eligible from their separation date until 365 days past end of tour of service -Discharged service members (if discharged honorably) and their immediate family members until 365 days past end of tour of service, retirement date or discharge date. Coast Guard veterans (if discharged honorably) and their immediate family are eligible from their separation date until 365 days past end of tour of service. -Designated Department of Defense (DOD) expeditionary civilians, as defined by Directive-type Memorandum (DTM)-17-004, “Department of Defense Expeditionary Civilian Workforce” during the 90 days prior to deployment and 180 days post-deployment. -Immediate family of designated DOD expeditionary civilians, including spouses, children and anyone who has legal responsibility for workforce member’s children, for the benefit of the children -Military academy cadets, students enrolled in one of the service academies for the Army, Navy or Air Force -Active-duty foreign military service members assigned to U.S. Army, Marine Corps, Navy, Air Force and Space Force installations who are registered in DEERS. Foreign military service members who have DOD-issued CAC with MWR/commissary/exchange privileges. -Immediate family members of foreign military active-duty service members registered in Defense Enrollment Eligibility Reporting System (DEERS). This includes spouse as defined by 10 USC Section 101 and children. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week (800) 342-9647 Visit Website More Details RETURNING VETERANS PROJECT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential mental and physical health services through volunteer health providers. Traditional and non-traditional services may be available. Eligibility: Veterans, Service Members, including National Guard and Reserve personnel, and Military Family members (including spouses/partners, children, parents, grandparents, and siblings directly impacted by military service). Hours: Varies Email (503) 954-2259 Visit Website More Details VA NORTHWEST HEALTH NETWORK PORTLAND VA MEDICAL CENTER HEALTH CARE SYSTEM COMMUNITY HOMELESS REINTEGRATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Community Reintegration Services (CRS) assist veterans who are homeless or at risk of homelessness in developing and maintaining the skills necessary to succeed in community living. Services include: -Vocational Services where veterans can access job search assistance, job coaching, educational guidance, and vocational skill-building resources -Community Shelter and Housing works with community providers to help homeless veterans find local shelters -Outreach to homeless veterans -Case Managed Housing maintains 160+ veterans in case-managed housing 3710 SW US Veterans Hospital Road Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Veterans who are homeless or at risk of homelessness Hours: Phone hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Walk-in housing program hours: Tuesday/Thursday 9am-3pm (503) 273-5187 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VA NORTHWEST HEALTH NETWORK PORTLAND VA MEDICAL CENTER HEALTH CARE SYSTEM VETERANS' JUSTICE OUTREACH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The purpose of the Veterans Justice Outreach (VJO) initiative is to avoid unnecessary criminalization of mental illness and extended incarceration among veterans by ensuring that eligible veterans in contact with the criminal justice system have access to Veterans Health Administration (VHA) mental health and substance abuse services. The VJO can assess needs and identify appropriate resources, refer and link qualified veterans to appropriate services, and liaise between VA and courts, attorneys, and community partners. The VJO cannot provide legal adcice, perfom forensic psychological evaluations for the cours, accept custody of a veteran, or guarantee VA program acceptance. 3710 SW US Veterans Hospital Road Portland, OR 97239 Eligibility: Justice-involved veterans Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-4pm (503) 721-1025 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VA NORTHWEST HEALTH NETWORK SOUTHERN OREGON VA REHABILITATION CENTER AND CLINICS VETERANS' JUSTICE OUTREACH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The purpose of the Veterans Justice Outreach (VJO) initiative is to avoid unnecessary criminalization of mental illness and extended incarceration among veterans by ensuring that eligible veterans in contact with the criminal justice system have access to Veterans Health Administration (VHA) mental health and substance abuse services. The VJO can assess needs and identify appropriate resources, refer and link qualified veterans to appropriate services, and liaise between VA and courts, attorneys, and community partners. The VJO cannot provide legal adcice, perfom forensic psychological evaluations for the cours, accept custody of a veteran, or guarantee VA program acceptance. 8495 Crater Lake Highway White City, OR 97503 Eligibility: Justice-involved veterans Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-4pm (541) 826-2111 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VA NORTHWEST HEALTH NETWORK VA ROSEBURG HEALTHCARE SYSTEM VETERANS' JUSTICE OUTREACH PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The purpose of the Veterans Justice Outreach (VJO) initiative is to avoid unnecessary criminalization of mental illness and extended incarceration among veterans by ensuring that eligible veterans in contact with the criminal justice system have access to Veterans Health Administration (VHA) mental health and substance abuse services. The VJO can assess needs and identify appropriate resources, refer and link qualified veterans to appropriate services, and liaise between VA and courts, attorneys, and community partners. The VJO cannot provide legal adcice, perfom forensic psychological evaluations for the cours, accept custody of a veteran, or guarantee VA program acceptance. 913 NW Garden Valley Boulevard Roseburg, OR 97471 Eligibility: Justice-involved veterans Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-4pm (541) 440-1000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VA NORTHWEST HEALTH NETWORK VETERAN REINTEGRATION CENTERS VETERAN REINTEGRATION SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a wide range of psychosocial services and counseling to support a successful transition from military to civilian life. Services provided include individual and group counseling for veterans, service members, and their families; family counseling for military-related issues; bereavement counseling for families who experience an active duty death; military sexual trauma counseling and referral; and outreach and education through programs like the Post-Deployment Health Reassessment Program. They also provide substance abuse assessment and referral, employment assessment and referral, explanations and referrals for Veterans Benefits Administration benefits, and screening and referrals for medical issues such as Traumatic Brain Injury and depression. Website Eligibility: Veterans and their family members Hours: Varies by location (877) 927-8387 877-- 247 Visit Website More Details EASTERSEALS OREGON HOMELESS VETERAN REINTEGRATION PROGRAM EASTERSEALS OREGON HOMELESS VETERAN REINTEGRATION PROGRAM EASTERSEALS OREGON HOMELESS VETERAN REINTEGRATION PROGRAM EASTERSEALS OREGON HOMELESS VETERAN REINTEGRATION PROGRAM EASTERSEALS OREGON HOMELESS VETERAN REINTEGRATION PROGRAM EASTERSEALS OREGON HOMELESS VETERAN REINTEGRATION PROGRAM VA NORTHWEST HEALTH NETWORK COMMUNITY HOMELESS REINTEGRATION SERVICES VA NORTHWEST HEALTH NETWORK VETERANS' JUSTICE OUTREACH PROGRAM VA NORTHWEST HEALTH NETWORK VETERANS' JUSTICE OUTREACH PROGRAM
